note: SNG-229 hunt quests, RE-AUTHORED CORRECTLY (Aevi 2026-07-22) after my first pass broke content_ci (invented givers, prose-only outcomes). Now: REAL NPC givers + machine-readable effects[]. STAGED not loaded because (1) creatureId resolves only once CCode fixes the provides.bestiary loader gap [current content_ci FAIL, CCode's §2a incomplete], (2) verify against the loaded bestiary before folding into quests.json. Drop into content/packs/valley/quests.json once the loader reads the bestiary. Schema matches quests.json exactly.

quests.name: The Stag That Won't Die
quests.premise: The wrong stag has taken three walkers at the tree-line, and the graze has been silent since it changed. Keeper Ilma, whose whole craft is the mercy of stopping, cannot end a thing already past dying-right — and it shames her. She asks you to do what Palework cannot. A Lattice broker will quietly pay for the humming antler.
quests.stakes: Three dead; the tree-line ground lost while it stands; and a keeper facing the one death she cannot give.
stages: Read what the stag has become (KNOW).
Corner it — it does not flee like a beast (MOVE).
End it — a kill, not a keeping (HARM).
routes: Hunt it clean for Ilma's peace
Take the antler for the broker's coin
Both, if you can stomach her face

outcomes.name: Ended Clean
outcomes.summary: The stag falls; the graze reopens.
narration: The wrong stag is put down. The tree-line ground is walkable again.
Keeper Ilma grieves the death she could not give — and trusts you with what her craft cannot reach.

effects.note: trusts you with the deaths her craft cannot keep

effects.fact: A thing past dying-right; ended by a hand where Palework could not reach.

outcomes.name: The Antler Taken
outcomes.summary: The stag falls; the humming antler changes hands.
narration: You end the stag and cut the lattice-antler free. A Lattice broker pays well and asks nothing.
Ilma sees the stump. She says nothing. She remembers.

effects.fact: Cut from the wrong stag; hums with something resolved wrong. A key, a proof, or a fortune to the right buyer.

quests.name: The Walk That Won't Stop
quests.premise: A hollow-pace still walks the line of a wall that fell a century ago, and it has begun 'defending' the gap against anyone who crosses. Maker Orrin wants it taken INTACT — its rotted directive is a readable pre-Transition record, worth more captured than broken. Everyone else on the road wants it gone.
quests.stakes: The crossing closed while it patrols; and a Wright's dread of a made thing outliving its purpose.
stages: Break the directive without destroying the core Orrin wants (UNMAKE).
End the frame — or bind it whole (HARM or WARD).
routes: Take it intact for Orrin's coin and knowledge

outcomes.name: Broken for the Road
outcomes.summary: The frame falls; the crossing reopens.
narration: You break the hollow-pace. The road opens. A made thing stops walking at last.
Orrin is disappointed — but a Wright understands ending a thing that outlived its purpose.

effects.fact: A made thing outliving maker and purpose. Broken, not studied.

outcomes.name: Taken Intact
outcomes.summary: Bound whole; Orrin reads a century-old directive.
narration: You bind the hollow-pace without breaking its core. Orrin reads its rotted directive — and goes very quiet.
Something in the record starts a new thread.

effects.note: in your debt; shares what the directive reveals

effects.seed: The rotted directive names a place, or a purpose, that should not still exist.

quests.name: The Water Gone Wrong
quests.premise: The warplings have gotten into the upper water, and the beasts that drink there move wrong now too. Reed Mother Ossa — watching her herd change — will trade coin or standing to have them cleared at the source before wrongness becomes the whole valley's problem.
quests.stakes: A spreading wrongness; a herd, then a valley, if unchecked.
stages: Trace the warplings to the source pool (KNOW).
Clear them — they cannot be healed back (HARM).

outcomes.name: Water Runs Right
narration: You clear the source and ward the water. The herd settles within days.
Ossa is in your debt, and the valley never learns how close it came.

effects.note: in your debt; the herd steadies

effects.fact: Life gone wrong past healing; cleared at the source before it spread.

outcomes.name: Rushed
narration: You kill the warplings but don't ward the pool. Ossa thanks you.
Weeks later, the wrongness seeps back — slower, and worse.

effects.seed: The wrongness returns to the upper water — a second, harder clearing.

quests.name: The Choir That Means Nothing
quests.premise: The unmoored choir is spreading — a drifting region of voices that mean nothing, expression severed from feeling, its edge closer than it was. To the old choirmaster it is obscene: their art, unmoored from meaning, growing. Epic ground — a campaign, not a strike, and it takes more than one hand.
quests.stakes: A domain-scale drift that will not stay put. Everything within its growing reach.
stages: Map the choir's growth; find what feeds it (KNOW).
Hold its edge — it must not spread while you work (WARD).
Reach its hollow center and unmake the drift (UNMAKE).

outcomes.name: The Drift Collapses
outcomes.summary: The choir is unmade at its center.
narration: You reach the hollow center and unmake it. The voices fall silent across the valley's edge.
The old choirmaster weeps — this time with meaning.

effects.fact: Expression severed from feeling, grown domain-scale; unmade at its hollow center.

outcomes.name: Contained, Not Ended
narration: You hold its edge but cannot reach the center alone. The drift stops spreading — for now.
It stands as a world-threat, waiting for a larger hand.

effects.note: becomes a standing epic-scale threat (ties the epic-arc system)
